title: salix × rugulosa
intro: Salix × rugulosa, also known as Willow-Whitebeam or Hybrid Whitebeam, is a hybrid plant species resulting from the cross between Salix spp. (Willow) and Sorbus spp. (Whitebeam or Rowan). It has been found to have medicinal properties, particularly in its bark, which contains salicin, a compound similar to aspirin, providing relief from pain and inflammation. The health benefits of Salix × rugulosa include its potential to reduce fever, alleviate headaches, and soothe digestive issues. Salix × rugulosa can be used in herbal preparations, such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, often used to treat various ailments, although its usage should be done under the guidance of a healthcare professional.
